description_long: This guard stone is a sculpted block featuring a group of four figures in high relief, carved from a reddish-purple stone and mounted against a pale, rectangular stone backing. The figures are dressed in detailed, classical attire with armor and cloaks, and they embrace each other in a tight, unified pose, suggesting a historical or commemorative function. The base is robust and rectangular, with additional stonework that appears weathered and slightly chipped, indicating age and exposure. The rear of the object is flat and unadorned, serving as a support or mounting surface, while the front and sides display the intricate sculptural work. The stone surface shows a mix of smooth and rough textures, with some areas polished by touch or weather, and others retaining a more granular finish. The overall impression is of a monumental, immovable object, likely serving both a protective and decorative role in an urban or historical setting.
description: A monumental guard stone featuring four embracing, armored figures carved in reddish stone, set against a pale stone backing. The base and backing are robust and weathered, with detailed sculptural work on the front and sides.
description_view_0: Front view showing the four detailed, embracing figures in high relief, with the pale stone backing and base visible.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the flat, unadorned stone backing, with only the edges of the figures and base visible.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the depth of the sculpted figures and the layered construction of the base and backing.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the grouping of the figures, the detailed carving, and the robust, weathered base.
attribute_location: Typically found at the corner of historical buildings, near entrances, or at the edge of plazas in urban or heritage sites, often adjacent to pedestrian pathways or building facades.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, flush with a wall or building corner, with the sculpted figures facing outward toward the street or pathway.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible smoothing and slight discoloration on protruding surfaces of the figures, likely from weathering and occasional touch; minor chipping and erosion at the base edges, probably from contact with foot traffic or maintenance equipment.
